steps.title: Extract roots from factored form
steps.content: From $f(x) = (x-10)(x+13)$, we identify the roots directly: $x = 10$ and $x = -13$. Expanding to $f(x) = x^2 + 3x - 130$ shows the leading coefficient is positive, so the parabola opens upward and has a minimum.

steps.title: Find the minimum using axis of symmetry
steps.content:
The minimum occurs at the axis of symmetry, which is the average of the two roots:

$$x = \frac{10 + (-13)}{2} = \frac{-3}{2}$$

This matches choice D — D is correct.
